prerequisites: An X (formerly Twitter) account that you can use to sign up for API access.
An X developer account created at the developer console (console.x.com), with the Developer Agreement and Policy accepted.
An App created in the developer console; the console issues the credentials used to authenticate, including the Bearer Token.
A Bearer Token from that App to populate the connector's required api_key field.
A search query string for the query config field (the filter that selects which posts to retrieve). This field is required.

Optional: start_date and end_date values if you want to bound the time window of results. The X recent search endpoint uses UTC timestamps in YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ format, and recent search only covers the last 7 days.

authMethods.name: Bearer Token (OAuth 2.0 App-Only)
authMethods.summary: The connector's api_key secret field holds an X API v2 Bearer Token. The X API authenticates app-only requests for reading public data by sending this token in the HTTP Authorization header (Authorization: Bearer <token>). The Bearer Token is generated for your App in the X developer console and does not act on behalf of an individual user. Treat it as a secret; the console displays credentials only once, so store it securely and regenerate if lost.

setupSteps.title: Sign up for an X developer account
setupSteps.body: Go to the X developer console (console.x.com) and authenticate with your X account. Review and accept the Developer Agreement and Policy, and complete your profile describing how you intend to use the API.

setupSteps.title: Create an App and copy the Bearer Token
setupSteps.body: In the developer console, create a new App. When the App is created, the console generates your credentials, including a Bearer Token used for app-only authentication to read public data. Copy the Bearer Token immediately; credentials are shown only once, so store it in a secure secret store. If lost, regenerate it (which invalidates the old one).

setupSteps.title: Provide the Bearer Token as api_key
setupSteps.body: Set the connector's required api_key field to the Bearer Token from your App. The connector sends it in the Authorization header to authenticate requests to the X API v2.

setupSteps.title: Set the required query
setupSteps.body: Set the required query field to the search filter that selects which posts to retrieve. This maps to the X API v2 recent search query parameter, which matches posts from the last 7 days and supports search filters up to 4,096 characters.

setupSteps.title: Optionally bound the time window with start_date and end_date
setupSteps.body: If you want to restrict results to a time range, set the optional start_date and end_date fields. These correspond to the X recent search start_time and end_time parameters, expressed as UTC timestamps in YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ format. start_time is inclusive (oldest) and end_time is exclusive (newest); both must fall within the last 7 days that recent search covers.

sources.title: Getting Access to the X API
X API v2 Recent Search endpoint (GET /2/tweets/search/recent)
